title: 2007-2021 Toyota Tundra Alternator Replacement Instructions - Also For Sequoia, Land Cruiser, LX570
description: Remove and replace the alternator on a 20007-2021 Toyota Tundra, including sway bar, power steering pump, and transmission oil cooler line bracket removal for access.
tips: Access the accessory belt tensioner from the bottom — it's very difficult to see from the top.
Apply penetrating oil to the power steering pump bracket to help it slide out.
Tap the metal insert on the power steering pump mount rearward to spread the gap and ease reinstallation; apply lubricant to the mating surfaces.

Unbolt the sway bar before attempting to remove the alternator — it's nearly impossible to extract from the bottom with it in place, and removal also improves ratchet access to the lower alternator bolts.
Remove the 12mm bolt holding the transmission oil cooler lines to the engine oil pan so the lines can be moved out of the way of the lower alternator bolts.
Disconnect the oil pressure sensor and nearby harness clips to gain enough clearance to extract the alternator.
Check the two alignment dowel sleeves after removal — they can stay stuck in the block or in the alternator; ensure one is seated at each location before reinstalling.
The alternator sits on a stud at the top, so the top bolt will come out but the alternator won't drop until pried off the stud.
Moving the power steering pump as far forward as possible before unbolting the alternator gives better access to the top nut and bolt.

torque_specs.fastener: Alternator bolts and nut (3 bolts, 1 nut)
